comment.body:
What I’m seeing is a short fly with 6825 as the highest gamma strike with negative speed above( but not so steep), anyway speed seems positive (spot up gamma up= spot up vol down) in addition charm seems negative above  5825 and positive  below and this creates a “gravitational field” around the high gamma strike line because of the charm convexity that I think will become steeper as time passes due to color. Maybe a pinning effect at the fly that trap the momentum as long as Vol remain low, if not we could see a spot up vol up toward 5900 as color could steeps the gamma profile .

Correct me if I’m wrong as always I’m trying to interpret it with creativity and based on my lil past experience here🤓

comment.body: My probably wrong interpretation please tell me all the things I'm getting wrong so we can all help each other learn : MM long fly centre at 5830, I assume we are in positive vanna from 5730 ish up, so if vol crushes it is supportive to 5830 where we could pin.  We would need higher vol above 5830 to go much further up and higher vol below to go significantly lower. Negative not particularly steep speed above centre 5830 and positive speed below.

comment.body: I know it's ahead of market open but I wanted to use this moment to ask a question as I've pored through all the articles and chats as much as possible to try and give myself a base of understanding. Previously I had been using quant data to look at gamma exposure and think of it as support/resistance or magnet levels to watch for market reactions in conjunction with a rising/falling VIX to trade SPX intraday. Following all the reading and trying to understand the complexity of the greeks has given my paralysis by overanalyzes and I've subsequently felt like I've lost my edge bc I'm second guessing everything I'm seeing with price action on multi-time frames as I had before. I'm really trying to use what I"m learning here to help build a trading plan but think I'm clearly missing something about how to structure my SPX trades and pick my strikes and targets with regards to risk:reward propositions. 3:1 was my normal target and I've eaten some losses lately. Any guidance? How to simplify and streamline what's being provided here in a basic sense even to go back to paper trading until I have it down more? I like the idea of the mean reversion and using the bands and levels for confluence for trades intraday but find myself second guessing a lot.

comment.body:
Short vol, short vomma is owned. But don't be complacent. Read my weekly post carefully and read between the lines!
I can’t say much, but the negotiations in Jeddah are making slow, steady progress. Our politicians are working through sensitive issues. To achieve Middle East peace, there must first be a stable U.S.–Russia relationship, because the Arabs respect Putin.

Funds are prepared for the next market event. As I’ve mentioned, the goal now is to re-inject liquidity into the market, prompting many bears to capitulate. Meanwhile, I’m using these opportunities to build a lifeboat.

It’s not over yet. This drop still isn’t enough to support a sustained QE

comment.body: The mean reversion levels are very useful for sure, do you have a vol bias (I definitely do). When the MM exposure points to pinning I naturally hope that it will break out even if the evidence doesn't point to that. Trying to take mean reversion trades on days like that can be costly because you'll keep getting stopped out with little chance of hitting 3:1 risk reward.
comment.raw_body: The mean reversion levels are very useful for sure, do you have a vol bias (I definitely do). comment.body:
Just talking it out with you and I'm definitely not an expert. But the daily post said "Coding 5748.69 as the lvl where if we see further vol crush, spot will be pinned to 5750. But if you see vol increase here, you can see a longer spot/vol beta move up towards 5773.88, but there if vol can decline, bears gonna be rekt hard. 
Basically, above 5688.5 one should see net vol decline, in order to see stabilization, if the customers are serious about their sold calls, and very important to see the vol decline to get realized not just implied!"
